senses.definition: a type of television or radio program in which the audience take part by calling the studio to voice their opinions

examples.x: The call-in show on the local radio station invites listeners to share their thoughts on current events and community issues.
She enjoys listening to the call-in program on politics, where people from all walks of life express their viewpoints.
The TV network launched a new call-in segment to engage viewers and encourage interactive participation.
The call-in segment of the talk show allows audience members to pose questions to the guest experts.
He dialed into the call-in radio program to share his personal experiences and offer advice to others in similar situations.

senses.definition: products such as movies, TV shows, etc. that are made to be educational as well as entertaining

examples.x: Many parents use edutainment tools to help their children learn new languages in an enjoyable way.
This podcast is a great example of edutainment, as it discusses important topics while keeping the listener engaged with humor and stories.
Edutainment programs are perfect for keeping kids entertained while also teaching them important life skills.
Some video games use edutainment to help players improve their problem-solving skills while having fun.

senses.definition: one part of a series of a radio or TV show

examples.x: The latest episode of the podcast delved into the history of ancient civilizations, captivating listeners with its engaging storytelling.
Fans eagerly awaited the premiere of the new episode of their favorite TV drama, eager to see how the plot would unfold.
Each episode of the documentary series explores a different aspect of wildlife conservation, shedding light on the efforts to protect endangered species.
The radio show's host introduced the guest for the evening's episode, promising an insightful discussion on current events and politics.

senses.definition: pre-recorded laughter added to a radio or television show to make it seem like the audience is having a laugh

examples.x: Some people find laugh tracks distracting or unnecessary.
The producers decided to add a laugh track to make the show feel more lively.
The laugh track helped fill the silence after every punchline.
The sitcom was funny, but the constant laugh track felt forced and distracting.

senses.definition: ‌a television show with a few episodes, shown over a short stretch of time

examples.x: I watched that miniseries last weekend, and it was really engaging from start to finish.
She recommended a great miniseries about history, which I plan to start tonight.
The miniseries got so much attention that they might turn it into a full TV show.
I prefer miniseries because they have a complete story without dragging on for seasons.

senses.definition: a TV or radio competition in which people play games or answer questions to win

examples.x: The panel show last night had some really interesting debates about technology.
She was invited to be a guest on a popular panel show this week.
That panel show always makes me laugh because of the witty remarks from the guests.
We should try to get tickets to see the live recording of the panel show.

senses.definition: a TV or radio program made to see how the audience react and whether it should be made into a series
an entertainment show on radio or television in which people compete to win prizes by answering a number of questions

examples.x: I love watching quiz shows on TV because they test my general knowledge.
He won a lot of money on a quiz show last night.
They are filming a new quiz show in the studio downtown.
Did you see that quiz show last week?
I enjoy watching quiz shows that challenge my general knowledge.

senses.definition: entertainment shows on television about the lives of ordinary people in real situations

examples.x: Some people think reality TV is fake, but I enjoy it for the entertainment.
Reality TV can be a bit over-the-top, but it's fun to watch when you need something light.
My sister loves reality TV shows, especially the ones about survival challenges.
Have you seen that new reality TV series where people live on an island?
A lot of reality TV shows have a lot of drama to keep the viewers hooked.

senses.definition: ‌a program on television or radio that has already been broadcast

examples.x: The network decided to air a repeat of last week's popular episode.
I missed the show last night, but luckily there's a repeat on Saturday.
During the holiday season, the channel often plays repeats of classic movies.
The radio station scheduled a repeat of the interview due to high demand.
She tuned in for the repeat of the documentary she missed earlier in the week.

senses.definition: the playback of a short part of a sports match, tape, etc. on television or radio
a series of episodes broadcast on television that tells a story or provides entertainment, usually consisting of a specific genre or format

examples.x: I love watching my favorite television show every evening after work.
We watched a television show about wildlife documentaries during dinner.
I can't wait for the next season of that crime television show to start

senses.definition: a TV or radio show, broadcast regularly, dealing with the routine life of a group of people and their problems

examples.x: She never misses an episode of her favorite soap opera.
The soap opera's dramatic twists keep viewers coming back for more.
He used to listen to soap operas on the radio with his grandmother.
The soap opera has been on air for over twenty years, captivating audiences with its ongoing storyline.
The characters' struggles in the soap opera feel so real and relatable to many viewers.

senses.definition: a program on television or radio that broadcasts news reports

examples.x: The newscast covered the latest updates on the storm approaching the coast.
I always watch the evening newscast to stay informed about the world.
The newscast began with a report on the local traffic situation.
We missed the newscast, so we had to catch up on the news online.
The newscast included an interview with the mayor about the city's plans for growth.

senses.definition: a broadcast on television or radio about sports events or sports news
a type of TV or radio program on which famous people appear as guests to answer questions about themselves or other subjects

examples.x: She appeared on a talk show to discuss her new book.
The talk show featured interviews with several celebrities.
His favorite talk show airs every evening at 7 PM.
They watched the talk show to hear the latest entertainment news.
A live audience attended the talk show to interact with the guests.

senses.definition: ‌a type of television program that is broadcast for several hours, aimed to collect money for charity

examples.x: The annual telethon raised millions of dollars for cancer research.
Celebrities joined forces for a telethon to support victims of natural disasters.
The telethon's goal was to raise awareness and funds for mental health initiatives.
Corporate sponsors contributed matching funds during the telethon to maximize donations.
Viewers tuned in to the telethon to donate to local food banks during the holiday season.

senses.definition: a type of TV or theatrical production that consists of several short performances such as singing, dancing, etc.

examples.x: The variety production at the theater included a mix of song, dance, and magic tricks, delighting the audience.
The variety program for the evening included stand-up comedy, acrobatics, and a live band performance.
Viewers tuned in every Saturday night to watch the beloved variety series that offered something for everyone.

senses.definition: a report on possible weather conditions and how they will change in the following day or days

examples.x: They rely on the weather forecast to plan outdoor events for their business.
The weather forecast says it will rain later, so don't forget your umbrella.
I always check the weather forecast in the morning before deciding what to wear.
According to the weather forecast, the weekend will be sunny and warm.
They relied on the weather forecast to prepare for the outdoor festival.

senses.definition: text displayed on a screen that shows the spoken words and sounds in a program for viewers who may not hear them
the immediate playback of an important moment in a sports competition on television, usually shown in slow motion

examples.x: The referee used an instant replay to check if the goal was offside.
After the incredible dunk, the announcer called for an instant replay to show the crowd.
During the basketball game, the instant replay helped confirm the foul was committed.
The soccer match paused for an instant replay of the controversial penalty kick.
The football team’s coach asked for an instant replay to review the touchdown.

senses.definition: spoken descriptions given in a movie or a television show, etc. by a narrator that is not seen by the audience
a recorded copy of a broadcasted radio or television program for reviewing and evaluation purposes
used to refer to program that is being broadcasted on radio or television

examples.x: Rockwell hopes the program can be on the air within a year.
The wacky series has been on the air for ten years.

senses.definition: the amount of time given to a program on television or radio

examples.x: The radio station schedules the news broadcast during peak airtime.
They are trying to get more airtime for their new podcast.
The station decided to shorten the airtime of the morning talk show.
The network bought additional airtime to promote their upcoming series.

senses.definition: a pause in a television or radio program during which advertisements or commercials are shown

examples.x: I usually grab a snack during the commercial break.
I muted the TV during the commercial break to avoid the loud ads.
The show was interrupted by a commercial break right after the exciting moment.
The host promised to reveal the answer after the commercial break.
As soon as the commercial break started, she rushed to grab a drink.

senses.definition: a spoken description of an event while it is taking place, particularly on TV or radio

examples.x: The sports commentator provided live commentary during the soccer match.
Listeners tuned in to the radio station for the expert commentary on the political debate.
The television broadcast featured insightful commentary from political analysts.
The live stream of the awards ceremony included colorful commentary from the hosts.
The nature documentary was enhanced by the engaging commentary of the narrator.

senses.definition: the arranged sequence of items in a television program or of performers in a team

examples.x: The host kept glancing at the running order to stay on track during the broadcast.
The running order was adjusted last minute when one of the guests arrived late.
The producer handed out the running order to everyone before the live show began.
The crew followed the running order to make sure the program stayed on schedule.
We had to memorize the running order so we knew exactly when our part would air.

senses.definition: a list of radio and television programs on a particular network and the exact time they start
the rebroadcast of a program on television or other media

examples.x: The cable channel airs reruns of old movies every weekend.
The rerun of the live concert drew a large audience eager to relive the experience.
The network scheduled a rerun of last season's finale due to popular demand.
She caught a rerun of her favorite cooking show while waiting at the airport.

senses.definition: a line of news headlines passing across a television or computer screen

examples.x: The news ticker on the TV showed updates about the ongoing storm in the area.
As I watched the sports game, the news ticker displayed scores from other matches.
During the broadcast, the news ticker constantly updated with new information about the stock market.
As I watched the movie, the news ticker kept flashing breaking news about the election results.

senses.definition: the process of scheduling programs that are planned to be broadcast on radio or television
television or radio content that is produced and aired specifically for a local audience, often focusing on news, events, and topics of local interest

examples.x: She loves watching the local programming on weekends to catch up on neighborhood events.
They decided to turn off the national channels and tune into the local programming for the latest weather updates.
The radio station focuses on local programming, featuring interviews with community leaders.
The station prides itself on its local programming, offering content that speaks directly to the community’s needs.

senses.definition: a company or building where TV or radio programs are being broadcast
a room or building where TV or radio programs are made or broadcast

examples.x: The TV studio bustled with activity as crew members prepared for the live broadcast of the morning news.
The radio studio featured state-of-the-art soundboards and recording booths, where DJs entertained listeners with music and talk shows.
Actors rehearsed their lines on the soundstage of the film studio, surrounded by elaborate sets and props.
Podcasters recorded episodes in the makeshift studio set up in their spare bedroom, using portable microphones and editing software.

senses.definition: a kind of radio broadcast in which a particular topic is discussed and listeners are encouraged to make a phone call and share their opinions

examples.x: I usually listen to talk radio in the morning while I'm getting ready for work.
The talk radio host was discussing the latest political debates when I tuned in.
My dad loves talk radio, especially the shows where listeners can call in with their opinions.
She called into a talk radio show to share her thoughts on the new policy changes.

senses.definition: a television or radio broadcast or program of the latest news

examples.x: I watch the evening news to stay informed about current events.
My dad is always watching the news.
The news channel provides real-time updates through its website and social media.

senses.definition: a brief news program that is broadcast on the radio or television

examples.x: The radio station interrupted its regular programming to deliver a breaking news bulletin.
The school sends out a weekly bulletin to keep parents informed about upcoming events.
The police issued a bulletin describing the suspect they were searching for.
The hospital posted a bulletin in the lobby with information about visiting hours.
The company's CEO addressed employees in a bulletin regarding the upcoming changes to the organization.

senses.definition: a large card that is held up behind a camera for presenters or anchors to read from while they seem to be looking at the camera

examples.x: The actor glanced at the cue card to remember his next line.
During the live broadcast, the host kept looking at the cue cards to stay on track.
The news anchor read from the cue card before delivering the important update.
The presenter used cue cards to ensure she didn’t forget any important points during the speech.

senses.definition: the transmission of television or radio programs that are funded through advertising revenue

examples.x: In commercial broadcasting, the more popular the program, the more expensive the advertising slots become.
I watched a movie on a commercial broadcasting network, and it was interrupted by several ads.
Commercial broadcasting has been criticized for focusing too much on ads instead of content.
Sports events on commercial broadcasting channels usually include a lot of advertisements during the breaks.

senses.definition: the estimated number of people who watch a TV show or listen to a radio program
the time at which the largest number of people are watching TV or listening to the radio

examples.x: Advertisers pay top dollar for commercials aired during prime time television slots.
The new sitcom premieres tonight during prime time, attracting viewers of all ages.
The network's prime time lineup includes popular dramas and reality shows.
She enjoys watching her favorite game show every evening during prime time.
The news anchor delivers the evening broadcast during prime time, reaching millions of viewers.

senses.definition: a feature provided in some digital devices (cellphones, television, etc.) that allows parents to have control over their children's access to unwanted content

examples.x: The streaming service lets you customize parental controls based on the type of content you want to limit.
He activated parental controls on his child's smartphone to make sure they only used educational apps.
You can use parental controls to block violent shows during prime time television.
The TV has a built-in feature that allows parents to set parental controls for different age groups.

senses.definition: a broadcasting system in which the viewer has to pay to watch a particular program

examples.x: The concert was available on pay-per-view, but I chose to watch it online for free.
We decided to buy the movie on pay-per-view instead of going to the theater.
The pay-per-view option for the Super Bowl is available on multiple streaming services this year.
I saw an ad for a pay-per-view event featuring a live comedy show this weekend.

senses.definition: a form of television broadcasting in which the viewer pays to have access to a particular program or channel

examples.x: She signed up for pay television to watch the latest blockbuster movies that aren't available on regular TV.
Many people prefer pay television because it offers a wide range of channels and on-demand content.
He decided to cancel his pay television subscription to save money, since he mostly streams shows online.
I’m thinking of upgrading my pay television package to get more channels.

senses.definition: a TV station that broadcasts different programs

examples.x: The local channel airs a mix of community events, weather updates, and locally produced programs.
Television networks compete for viewership by offering exclusive programs and innovative channel packages.

senses.definition: a pre-recorded segment that is added into a live or recorded program, such as an advertisement or news update
the storytelling techniques extending a narrative across multiple platforms, such as movies, television shows, books, and video games, creating a cohesive experience for audiences
a type of content that combines information and entertainment

examples.x: Many news programs nowadays lean towards infotainment.
Some critics argue that infotainment trivializes important issues.
The rise of infotainment has transformed the media landscape.
The line between journalism and infotainment is becoming increasingly blurred.

senses.definition: a humorous show on television or radio with the same characters being involved with numerous funny situations in different episodes

examples.x: They spent the evening watching their favorite sitcom, laughing at every joke.
The sitcom had a quirky cast of characters that made every episode hilarious.
His go-to comfort show is a classic sitcom from the '90s.
The sitcom follows a group of friends living in New York City.
The actor became famous for his role in a popular sitcom.

senses.definition: a service delivering written news and information through television, currently replaced by other information services provided on a television network

examples.x: The decline of teletext began with the rise of the internet and digital broadcasting technologies.
Some countries still use teletext for public service announcements and emergency alerts on television channels.
